npcs.name: Gorren the Smith
dialog: Steel for the wilds, friend. A slime will laugh off your fists.
The iron sword holds an edge twice as long as that rusty thing.

npcs.name: Mara the Provisioner
dialog: Bread for the road, potions for when the road bites back.
Wolves hunt in packs out there. Carry more than you think you need.

npcs.name: Zella the Arcanist
dialog: A staff decides what you are: fire, frost, or mercy.
Casting takes focus — stand still, or the spell slips away.

npcs.name: Selvara the Enchanter
dialog: Every blade remembers what it was promised. I simply... remind it.
A finer piece holds more of my work — and holds it stronger.
I weave to the second degree here. For the third, seek the ember-witch past the desert.
Tired of an old weaving? I can unpick it — curses too, for a price.

npcs.name: Jib the Carpenter
dialog: Block by block — that's how anything worth keeping gets built.
Take the west portal to the Building Grounds and stack whatever you like. Torches too, for the night work.

npcs.name: Gate Guard
dialog: That portal leads to the Whispering Forest. Slimes near the road, wolves in the deep wood, bandits camped up north.
Die out there and your gear stays where you fell. Run back quick before someone else finds it.
There's a gate at the far end of the forest now — the Fen Gate. That one's for veterans, friend. Level eight or don't bother; the Gloomfen doesn't give bodies back.
Past the deep desert they've opened the Cinderrift. Ash, lava, and the forge-dead still working their shifts. Bring rift-forged steel or bring a will.
And if you've beaten the Gravelord — the crypt has a lower door. The Vaults of Morvane. Whatever's down there, the Gravelord was standing guard OVER it. Think on that.

npcs.name: Townsfolk
dialog: Lovely day on the plaza, isn't it?

npcs.name: Townsfolk
dialog: They say the bandits up north hoard fine steel.

npcs.name: Townsfolk
dialog: I once saw an epic blade drop from a common thief. Luck of the roll!
